results.possibleIdentity: Emilienne "Emily" Carson (1926–2016) and Isobel Hesse (died 2011) were graphic and exhibit designers based in Nyack, New York. Active during the late 1960s and early 1970s, they operated a design firm called 4 Designers Ltd. (also referred to as Four Designers Ltd.). Their professional work encompassed graphic design, exhibit design, and the production of film strips. They were associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded in 1966 by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to facilitate collaborations between artists and technical professionals. Both women were longtime residents of Rockland County; Emilienne Carson lived and ran her business in Tappan and Nyack, while Isobel Hesse was a prominent community figure who served as a Village Trustee in Nyack and was involved in local planning and waterfront revitalization efforts.
